authors_text: Anpei Chen,Zexiang Xu,Andreas Geiger,Jingyi Yu,Hao Su
title: TensoRF: Tensorial Radiance Fields
title_zh: TensoRF：张量辐射场
abstract: We present TensoRF, a novel approach to model and reconstruct radiance fields. Unlike NeRF that purely uses MLPs, we model the radiance field of a scene as a 4D tensor, which represents a 3D voxel grid with per-voxel multi-channel features. Our central idea is to factorize the 4D scene tensor into multiple compact low-rank tensor components. We demonstrate that applying traditional CP decomposition -- that factorizes tensors into rank-one components with compact vectors -- in our framework leads to improvements over vanilla NeRF. To further boost performance, we introduce a novel vector-matrix (VM) decomposition that relaxes the low-rank constraints for two modes of a tensor and factorizes tensors into compact vector and matrix factors. Beyond superior rendering quality, our models with CP and VM decompositions lead to a significantly lower memory footprint in comparison to previous and concurrent works that directly optimize per-voxel features. Experimentally, we demonstrate that TensoRF with CP decomposition achieves fast reconstruction (<30 min) with better rendering quality and even a smaller model size (<4 MB) compared to NeRF. Moreover, TensoRF with VM decomposition further boosts rendering quality and outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods, while reducing the reconstruction time (<10 min) and retaining a compact model size (<75 MB).
summary: We present TensoRF, a novel approach to model and reconstruct radiance fields. Unlike NeRF that purely uses MLPs, we model the radiance field of a scene as a 4D tensor, which represents a 3D voxel grid with per-voxel multi-channel features. Our central idea is to factorize the 4D scene tensor into multiple compact low-rank tensor components. We demonstrate that applying traditional CP decomposition -- that factorizes tensors into rank-one components with compact vectors -- in our framework leads to improvements over vanilla NeRF. To further boost performance, we introduce a novel vector-matrix (VM) decomposition that relaxes the low-rank constraints for two modes of a tensor and factorizes tensors into compact vector and matrix factors. Beyond superior rendering quality, our models with CP and VM decompositions lead to a significantly lower memory footprint in comparison to previous and concurrent works that directly optimize per-voxel features. Experimentally, we demonstrate that TensoRF with CP decomposition achieves fast reconstruction (<30 min) with better rendering quality and even a smaller model size (<4 MB) compared to NeRF. Moreover, TensoRF with VM decomposition further boosts rendering quality and outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods, while reducing the reconstruction time (<10 min) and retaining a compact model size (<75 MB).
abstract_zh: 我们提出 TensoRF，一种建模和重建辐射场的新方法。与纯粹使用 MLP 的 NeRF 不同，我们将场景的辐射场建模为 4D 张量，它表示具有每体素多通道特征的 3D 体素网格。我们的中心思想是将 4D 场景张量分解为多个紧凑的低秩张量分量。我们证明，在我们的框架中应用传统的 CP 分解（将张量分解为具有紧凑向量的一级分量）可以带来对普通 NeRF 的改进。为了进一步提高性能，我们引入了一种新颖的向量矩阵（VM）分解，它放宽了张量两种模式的低秩约束，并将张量分解为紧凑的向量和矩阵因子。除了卓越的渲染质量之外，与直接优化每体素特征的先前和并发工作相比，我们具有 CP 和 VM 分解的模型显着降低了内存占用。通过实验，我们证明与 NeRF 相比，具有 CP 分解的 TensoRF 可实现快速重建（<30 分钟），具有更好的渲染质量，甚至更小的模型大小（<4 MB）。此外，具有 VM 分解的 TensoRF 进一步提高了渲染质量并优于以前最先进的方法，同时减少了重建时间（<10 分钟）并保持紧凑的模型大小（<75 MB）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TensoRF，一种通过张量分解方法建模和重建辐射场的新技术，替代传统NeRF中的MLP结构。
AI.motivation: 传统NeRF使用MLP导致计算成本高、内存消耗大，本文旨在通过张量分解实现更高效的辐射场建模。
AI.method: 将场景表示为4D张量并分解为低秩张量组件，提出CP分解和改进的向量-矩阵（VM）分解方法以降低内存占用。
AI.result: CP分解实现<30分钟重建、<4MB模型大小，VM分解进一步提升渲染质量并缩短至<10分钟，模型保持紧凑。
AI.conclusion: TensoRF通过张量分解显著优化了辐射场重建的速度、质量和内存效率，优于现有方法。

motivation: 传统NeRF使用MLP导致计算成本高、内存消耗大，本文旨在通过张量分解实现更高效的辐射场建模。
method: 将场景表示为4D张量并分解为低秩张量组件，提出CP分解和改进的向量-矩阵（VM）分解方法以降低内存占用。
result: CP分解实现<30分钟重建、<4MB模型大小，VM分解进一步提升渲染质量并缩短至<10分钟，模型保持紧凑。
conclusion: TensoRF通过张量分解显著优化了辐射场重建的速度、质量和内存效率，优于现有方法。
search_text: TensoRF: Tensorial Radiance Fields TensoRF：张量辐射场 We present TensoRF, a novel approach to model and reconstruct radiance fields. Unlike NeRF that purely uses MLPs, we model the radiance field of a scene as a 4D tensor, which represents a 3D voxel grid with per-voxel multi-channel features. Our central idea is to factorize the 4D scene tensor into multiple compact low-rank tensor components. We demonstrate that applying traditional CP decomposition -- that factorizes tensors into rank-one components with compact vectors -- in our framework leads to improvements over vanilla NeRF. To further boost performance, we introduce a novel vector-matrix (VM) decomposition that relaxes the low-rank constraints for two modes of a tensor and factorizes tensors into compact vector and matrix factors. Beyond superior rendering quality, our models with CP and VM decompositions lead to a significantly lower memory footprint in comparison to previous and concurrent works that directly optimize per-voxel features. Experimentally, we demonstrate that TensoRF with CP decomposition achieves fast reconstruction (<30 min) with better rendering quality and even a smaller model size (<4 MB) compared to NeRF. Moreover, TensoRF with VM decomposition further boosts rendering quality and outperforms previous state-of-the-art methods, while reducing the reconstruction time (<10 min) and retaining a compact model size (<75 MB). 我们提出 TensoRF，一种建模和重建辐射场的新方法。与纯粹使用 MLP 的 NeRF 不同，我们将场景的辐射场建模为 4D 张量，它表示具有每体素多通道特征的 3D 体素网格。我们的中心思想是将 4D 场景张量分解为多个紧凑的低秩张量分量。我们证明，在我们的框架中应用传统的 CP 分解（将张量分解为具有紧凑向量的一级分量）可以带来对普通 NeRF 的改进。为了进一步提高性能，我们引入了一种新颖的向量矩阵（VM）分解，它放宽了张量两种模式的低秩约束，并将张量分解为紧凑的向量和矩阵因子。除了卓越的渲染质量之外，与直接优化每体素特征的先前和并发工作相比，我们具有 CP 和 VM 分解的模型显着降低了内存占用。通过实验，我们证明与 NeRF 相比，具有 CP 分解的 TensoRF 可实现快速重建（<30 分钟），具有更好的渲染质量，甚至更小的模型大小（<4 MB）。此外，具有 VM 分解的 TensoRF 进一步提高了渲染质量并优于以前最先进的方法，同时减少了重建时间（<10 分钟）并保持紧凑的模型大小（<75 MB）。 本文提出TensoRF，一种通过张量分解方法建模和重建辐射场的新技术，替代传统NeRF中的MLP结构。 Anpei Chen,Zexiang Xu,Andreas Geiger,Jingyi Yu,Hao Su cs.CV

authors_text: Paritosh Mittal,Yen-Chi Cheng,Maneesh Singh,Shubham Tulsiani
title: AutoSDF: Shape Priors for 3D Completion, Reconstruction and Generation
title_zh: AutoSDF：用于 3D 完成、重建和生成的形状先验
comment: In CVPR 2022. The first two authors contributed equally to this work. Project: https://yccyenchicheng.github.io/AutoSDF/. Add Supp
comments: In CVPR 2022. The first two authors contributed equally to this work. Project: https://yccyenchicheng.github.io/AutoSDF/. Add Supp
abstract: Powerful priors allow us to perform inference with insufficient information. In this paper, we propose an autoregressive prior for 3D shapes to solve multimodal 3D tasks such as shape completion, reconstruction, and generation. We model the distribution over 3D shapes as a non-sequential autoregressive distribution over a discretized, low-dimensional, symbolic grid-like latent representation of 3D shapes. This enables us to represent distributions over 3D shapes conditioned on information from an arbitrary set of spatially anchored query locations and thus perform shape completion in such arbitrary settings (e.g., generating a complete chair given only a view of the back leg). We also show that the learned autoregressive prior can be leveraged for conditional tasks such as single-view reconstruction and language-based generation. This is achieved by learning task-specific naive conditionals which can be approximated by light-weight models trained on minimal paired data. We validate the effectiveness of the proposed method using both quantitative and qualitative evaluation and show that the proposed method outperforms the specialized state-of-the-art methods trained for individual tasks. The project page with code and video visualizations can be found at https://yccyenchicheng.github.io/AutoSDF/.
summary: Powerful priors allow us to perform inference with insufficient information. In this paper, we propose an autoregressive prior for 3D shapes to solve multimodal 3D tasks such as shape completion, reconstruction, and generation. We model the distribution over 3D shapes as a non-sequential autoregressive distribution over a discretized, low-dimensional, symbolic grid-like latent representation of 3D shapes. This enables us to represent distributions over 3D shapes conditioned on information from an arbitrary set of spatially anchored query locations and thus perform shape completion in such arbitrary settings (e.g., generating a complete chair given only a view of the back leg). We also show that the learned autoregressive prior can be leveraged for conditional tasks such as single-view reconstruction and language-based generation. This is achieved by learning task-specific naive conditionals which can be approximated by light-weight models trained on minimal paired data. We validate the effectiveness of the proposed method using both quantitative and qualitative evaluation and show that the proposed method outperforms the specialized state-of-the-art methods trained for individual tasks. The project page with code and video visualizations can be found at https://yccyenchicheng.github.io/AutoSDF/.
abstract_zh: 强大的先验使我们能够在信息不足的情况下进行推理。在本文中，我们提出了 3D 形状的自回归先验来解决多模态 3D 任务，例如形状完成、重建和生成。我们将 3D 形状的分布建模为 3D 形状的离散、低维、符号网格状潜在表示上的非序列自回归分布。这使我们能够根据来自任意一组空间锚定查询位置的信息来表示 3D 形状的分布，从而在此类任意设置中执行形状补全（例如，仅给出后腿的视图生成一把完整的椅子）。我们还表明，学习到的自回归先验可以用于条件任务，例如单视图重建和基于语言的生成。这是通过学习特定于任务的朴素条件来实现的，这些条件可以通过在最小配对数据上训练的轻量级模型来近似。我们使用定量和定性评估验证了所提出方法的有效性，并表明所提出的方法优于针对单个任务训练的专门最先进的方法。包含代码和视频可视化的项目页面可以在 https://yccyenchiheng.github.io/AutoSDF/ 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于自回归先验的3D形状建模方法AutoSDF，用于解决形状补全、重建和生成等多模态3D任务。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理不完整或稀疏的3D信息时表现不足，需设计更强大的先验模型以支持多任务场景下的形状推理。
AI.method: 通过离散化低维符号网格表示建模3D形状分布，利用非序列自回归模型学习形状先验，并基于空间锚定查询位置实现条件生成与补全。
AI.result: 在定量与定性评估中，该方法优于针对单一任务优化的最先进方法，支持单视角重建与语言引导生成等条件任务。
AI.conclusion: AutoSDF通过统一的形状先验框架有效解决了多模态3D任务，展示了自回归模型在几何建模中的广泛适用性。

authors_text: Yuzhe Yang,Hao Wang,Dina Katabi
title: On Multi-Domain Long-Tailed Recognition, Imbalanced Domain Generalization and Beyond
title_zh: 多域长尾识别、不平衡域泛化及其他
abstract: Real-world data often exhibit imbalanced label distributions. Existing studies on data imbalance focus on single-domain settings, i.e., samples are from the same data distribution. However, natural data can originate from distinct domains, where a minority class in one domain could have abundant instances from other domains. We formalize the task of Multi-Domain Long-Tailed Recognition (MDLT), which learns from multi-domain imbalanced data, addresses label imbalance, domain shift, and divergent label distributions across domains, and generalizes to all domain-class pairs. We first develop the domain-class transferability graph, and show that such transferability governs the success of learning in MDLT. We then propose BoDA, a theoretically grounded learning strategy that tracks the upper bound of transferability statistics, and ensures balanced alignment and calibration across imbalanced domain-class distributions. We curate five MDLT benchmarks based on widely-used multi-domain datasets, and compare BoDA to twenty algorithms that span different learning strategies. Extensive and rigorous experiments verify the superior performance of BoDA. Further, as a byproduct, BoDA establishes new state-of-the-art on Domain Generalization benchmarks, highlighting the importance of addressing data imbalance across domains, which can be crucial for improving generalization to unseen domains. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/YyzHarry/multi-domain-imbalance.
summary: Real-world data often exhibit imbalanced label distributions. Existing studies on data imbalance focus on single-domain settings, i.e., samples are from the same data distribution. However, natural data can originate from distinct domains, where a minority class in one domain could have abundant instances from other domains. We formalize the task of Multi-Domain Long-Tailed Recognition (MDLT), which learns from multi-domain imbalanced data, addresses label imbalance, domain shift, and divergent label distributions across domains, and generalizes to all domain-class pairs. We first develop the domain-class transferability graph, and show that such transferability governs the success of learning in MDLT. We then propose BoDA, a theoretically grounded learning strategy that tracks the upper bound of transferability statistics, and ensures balanced alignment and calibration across imbalanced domain-class distributions. We curate five MDLT benchmarks based on widely-used multi-domain datasets, and compare BoDA to twenty algorithms that span different learning strategies. Extensive and rigorous experiments verify the superior performance of BoDA. Further, as a byproduct, BoDA establishes new state-of-the-art on Domain Generalization benchmarks, highlighting the importance of addressing data imbalance across domains, which can be crucial for improving generalization to unseen domains. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/YyzHarry/multi-domain-imbalance.
abstract_zh: 现实世界的数据通常表现出不平衡的标签分布。现有的数据不平衡研究主要集中在单域设置，即样本来自相同的数据分布。然而，自然数据可以源自不同的领域，其中一个领域中的少数类可能拥有来自其他领域的丰富实例。我们形式化了多域长尾识别（MDLT）的任务，它从多域不平衡数据中学习，解决标签不平衡、域转移和跨域的不同标签分布，并推广到所有域类对。我们首先开发了领域类可迁移性图，并表明这种可迁移性决定了 MDLT 学习的成功。然后，我们提出了 BoDA，一种基于理论的学习策略，可跟踪可转移性统计的上限，并确保跨不平衡的域类分布的平衡对齐和校准。我们根据广泛使用的多领域数据集策划了五个 MDLT 基准，并将 BoDA 与涵盖不同学习策略的二十种算法进行比较。广泛而严格的实验验证了BoDA的优越性能。此外，作为副产品，BoDA 建立了新的最先进的领域泛化基准，强调了解决跨领域数据不平衡的重要性，这对于提高对未知领域的泛化至关重要。代码和数据可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/YyzHarry/multi-domain-imbalance。

AI.tldr: 提出多领域长尾识别任务（MDLT），解决跨领域不平衡数据中的标签不平衡、领域偏移和标签分布差异问题，提升模型在未见领域的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有研究聚焦单一领域不平衡数据，但实际场景中数据来自不同领域，传统方法难以处理跨领域标签分布差异与领域偏移问题。
AI.method: 构建领域-类别可迁移性图，提出BoDA方法通过跟踪可迁移性统计量上界，实现跨领域不平衡分布下的平衡对齐与校准，并建立五个MDLT基准数据集。
AI.result: BoDA在五个MDLT基准及领域泛化任务中均优于20种对比算法，达到领域泛化新SOTA，验证了跨领域不平衡处理的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 跨领域数据不平衡问题对模型泛化能力至关重要，BoDA通过理论指导的方法有效解决了MDLT挑战，为领域泛化研究提供新方向。

Extensive and rigorous experiments verify the superior performance of BoDA. Further, as a byproduct, BoDA establishes new state-of-the-art on Domain Generalization benchmarks, highlighting the importance of addressing data imbalance across domains, which can be crucial for improving generalization to unseen domains. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/YyzHarry/multi-domain-imbalance. 现实世界的数据通常表现出不平衡的标签分布。现有的数据不平衡研究主要集中在单域设置，即样本来自相同的数据分布。然而，自然数据可以源自不同的领域，其中一个领域中的少数类可能拥有来自其他领域的丰富实例。我们形式化了多域长尾识别（MDLT）的任务，它从多域不平衡数据中学习，解决标签不平衡、域转移和跨域的不同标签分布，并推广到所有域类对。我们首先开发了领域类可迁移性图，并表明这种可迁移性决定了 MDLT 学习的成功。然后，我们提出了 BoDA，一种基于理论的学习策略，可跟踪可转移性统计的上限，并确保跨不平衡的域类分布的平衡对齐和校准。我们根据广泛使用的多领域数据集策划了五个 MDLT 基准，并将 BoDA 与涵盖不同学习策略的二十种算法进行比较。广泛而严格的实验验证了BoDA的优越性能。此外，作为副产品，BoDA 建立了新的最先进的领域泛化基准，强调了解决跨领域数据不平衡的重要性，这对于提高对未知领域的泛化至关重要。代码和数据可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/YyzHarry/multi-domain-imbalance。 提出多领域长尾识别任务（MDLT），解决跨领域不平衡数据中的标签不平衡、领域偏移和标签分布差异问题，提升模型在未见领域的泛化能力。 Yuzhe Yang,Hao Wang,Dina Katabi cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Jinhyung Park,Chenfeng Xu,Yiyang Zhou,Masayoshi Tomizuka,Wei Zhan
title: DetMatch: Two Teachers are Better Than One for Joint 2D and 3D Semi-Supervised Object Detection
title_zh: DetMatch：两位老师比一位老师更适合联合 2D 和 3D 半监督目标检测
abstract: While numerous 3D detection works leverage the complementary relationship between RGB images and point clouds, developments in the broader framework of semi-supervised object recognition remain uninfluenced by multi-modal fusion. Current methods develop independent pipelines for 2D and 3D semi-supervised learning despite the availability of paired image and point cloud frames. Observing that the distinct characteristics of each sensor cause them to be biased towards detecting different objects, we propose DetMatch, a flexible framework for joint semi-supervised learning on 2D and 3D modalities. By identifying objects detected in both sensors, our pipeline generates a cleaner, more robust set of pseudo-labels that both demonstrates stronger performance and stymies single-modality error propagation. Further, we leverage the richer semantics of RGB images to rectify incorrect 3D class predictions and improve localization of 3D boxes. Evaluating on the challenging KITTI and Waymo datasets, we improve upon strong semi-supervised learning methods and observe higher quality pseudo-labels. Code will be released at https://github.com/Divadi/DetMatch
summary: While numerous 3D detection works leverage the complementary relationship between RGB images and point clouds, developments in the broader framework of semi-supervised object recognition remain uninfluenced by multi-modal fusion. Current methods develop independent pipelines for 2D and 3D semi-supervised learning despite the availability of paired image and point cloud frames. Observing that the distinct characteristics of each sensor cause them to be biased towards detecting different objects, we propose DetMatch, a flexible framework for joint semi-supervised learning on 2D and 3D modalities. By identifying objects detected in both sensors, our pipeline generates a cleaner, more robust set of pseudo-labels that both demonstrates stronger performance and stymies single-modality error propagation. Further, we leverage the richer semantics of RGB images to rectify incorrect 3D class predictions and improve localization of 3D boxes. Evaluating on the challenging KITTI and Waymo datasets, we improve upon strong semi-supervised learning methods and observe higher quality pseudo-labels. Code will be released at https://github.com/Divadi/DetMatch
abstract_zh: 虽然许多 3D 检测工作利用了 RGB 图像和点云之间的互补关系，但更广泛的半监督对象识别框架的发展仍然不受多模态融合的影响。尽管存在配对图像和点云帧，但当前方法仍为 2D 和 3D 半监督学习开发独立的管道。观察到每个传感器的独特特征导致它们偏向于检测不同的物体，我们提出了 DetMatch，一种用于 2D 和 3D 模态联合半监督学习的灵活框架。通过识别两个传感器中检测到的物体，我们的管道生成了一组更清晰、更强大的伪标签，它们既表现出更强的性能，又阻止了单模态错误传播。此外，我们利用 RGB 图像更丰富的语义来纠正不正确的 3D 类预测并改进 3D 框的定位。通过评估具有挑战性的 KITTI 和 Waymo 数据集，我们改进了强大的半监督学习方法并观察到更高质量的伪标签。代码将在 https://github.com/Divadi/DetMatch 发布

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DetMatch的框架，用于联合2D和3D半监督目标检测。
AI.motivation: 当前的2D和3D半监督学习方法通常是独立进行的，而未充分利用图像和点云之间的互补性。由于不同传感器对检测对象有不同偏倚，因此需要一种能够结合多模态信息的框架。
AI.method: DetMatch通过识别在两个传感器中都检测到的对象，生成更干净和鲁棒的伪标签，并利用RGB图像的丰富语义来修正3D分类预测和提高3D框的定位。
AI.result: 在KITTI和Waymo数据集上评估，DetMatch优于现有的半监督学习方法，并生成了更高质量的伪标签。
AI.conclusion: DetMatch是一种灵活的框架，能够有效结合2D和3D信息，提升半监督目标检测的性能。

authors_text: Wen Wang,Jing Zhang,Yang Cao,Yongliang Shen,Dacheng Tao
title: Towards Data-Efficient Detection Transformers
title_zh: 迈向数据高效的检测变压器
abstract: Detection Transformers have achieved competitive performance on the sample-rich COCO dataset. However, we show most of them suffer from significant performance drops on small-size datasets, like Cityscapes. In other words, the detection transformers are generally data-hungry. To tackle this problem, we empirically analyze the factors that affect data efficiency, through a step-by-step transition from a data-efficient RCNN variant to the representative DETR. The empirical results suggest that sparse feature sampling from local image areas holds the key. Based on this observation, we alleviate the data-hungry issue of existing detection transformers by simply alternating how key and value sequences are constructed in the cross-attention layer, with minimum modifications to the original models. Besides, we introduce a simple yet effective label augmentation method to provide richer supervision and improve data efficiency. Experiments show that our method can be readily applied to different detection transformers and improve their performance on both small-size and sample-rich datasets. Code will be made publicly available at \url{https://github.com/encounter1997/DE-DETRs}.
summary: Detection Transformers have achieved competitive performance on the sample-rich COCO dataset. However, we show most of them suffer from significant performance drops on small-size datasets, like Cityscapes. In other words, the detection transformers are generally data-hungry. To tackle this problem, we empirically analyze the factors that affect data efficiency, through a step-by-step transition from a data-efficient RCNN variant to the representative DETR. The empirical results suggest that sparse feature sampling from local image areas holds the key. Based on this observation, we alleviate the data-hungry issue of existing detection transformers by simply alternating how key and value sequences are constructed in the cross-attention layer, with minimum modifications to the original models. Besides, we introduce a simple yet effective label augmentation method to provide richer supervision and improve data efficiency. Experiments show that our method can be readily applied to different detection transformers and improve their performance on both small-size and sample-rich datasets. Code will be made publicly available at \url{https://github.com/encounter1997/DE-DETRs}.
abstract_zh: 检测 Transformers 在样本丰富的 COCO 数据集上取得了具有竞争力的性能。然而，我们发现它们中的大多数在小型数据集（例如 Cityscapes）上都遭受了显着的性能下降。换句话说，检测变压器通常需要大量数据。为了解决这个问题，我们通过从数据高效的 RCNN 变体到代表性 DETR 的逐步过渡，实证分析了影响数据效率的因素。实证结果表明，局部图像区域的稀疏特征采样是关键。基于这一观察，我们通过简单地改变交叉注意力层中键和值序列的构造方式来缓解现有检测变压器的数据匮乏问题，并对原始模型进行最小程度的修改。此外，我们引入了一种简单而有效的标签增强方法，以提供更丰富的监督并提高数据效率。实验表明，我们的方法可以很容易地应用于不同的检测变压器，并提高它们在小规模和样本丰富的数据集上的性能。代码将在 \url{https://github.com/encounter1997/DE-DETRs} 上公开发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进检测Transformer数据效率的方法，通过优化特征采样和标签增强策略，解决模型在小数据集上性能下降的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有检测Transformer在大规模数据集（如COCO）表现优异，但在小数据集（如Cityscapes）上性能显著下降，暴露出发数据需求高的缺陷。
AI.method: 通过分析特征采样模式，改进交叉注意力层的键值序列构建方式，并提出简单有效的标签增强方法以提升监督信息质量。
AI.result: 方法可通用应用于多种检测Transformer架构，在小数据集和大数据集上均实现性能提升，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 稀疏局部特征采样是提升数据效率的关键，所提方法以最小模型修改实现了跨数据规模的性能优化。

authors_text: Hanxiao Tan
title: Visualizing Global Explanations of Point Cloud DNNs
title_zh: 可视化点云 DNN 的全局解释
abstract: In the field of autonomous driving and robotics, point clouds are showing their excellent real-time performance as raw data from most of the mainstream 3D sensors. Therefore, point cloud neural networks have become a popular research direction in recent years. So far, however, there has been little discussion about the explainability of deep neural networks for point clouds. In this paper, we propose a point cloud-applicable explainability approach based on a local surrogate model-based method to show which components contribute to the classification. Moreover, we propose quantitative fidelity validations for generated explanations that enhance the persuasive power of explainability and compare the plausibility of different existing point cloud-applicable explainability methods. Our new explainability approach provides a fairly accurate, more semantically coherent and widely applicable explanation for point cloud classification tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/Explain3D/LIME-3D
summary: In the field of autonomous driving and robotics, point clouds are showing their excellent real-time performance as raw data from most of the mainstream 3D sensors. Therefore, point cloud neural networks have become a popular research direction in recent years. So far, however, there has been little discussion about the explainability of deep neural networks for point clouds. In this paper, we propose a point cloud-applicable explainability approach based on a local surrogate model-based method to show which components contribute to the classification. Moreover, we propose quantitative fidelity validations for generated explanations that enhance the persuasive power of explainability and compare the plausibility of different existing point cloud-applicable explainability methods. Our new explainability approach provides a fairly accurate, more semantically coherent and widely applicable explanation for point cloud classification tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/Explain3D/LIME-3D
abstract_zh: 在自动驾驶和机器人领域，点云作为大多数主流3D传感器的原始数据，正在展示其出色的实时性能。因此，点云神经网络成为近年来的热门研究方向。然而，到目前为止，关于点云深度神经网络的可解释性的讨论还很少。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于局部代理模型的点云适用的可解释性方法，以显示哪些组件有助于分类。此外，我们提出对生成的解释进行定量保真度验证，以增强可解释性的说服力，并比较不同现有点云适用的可解释性方法的合理性。我们新的可解释性方法为点云分类任务提供了相当准确、语义更连贯且广泛适用的解释。我们的代码位于 https://github.com/Explain3D/LIME-3D

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种适用于点云深度神经网络的可解释性方法，用于可视化其分类决策的全局解释。
AI.motivation: 点云作为自动驾驶和机器人领域的主要3D传感器数据，其神经网络的可解释性研究尚不充分，亟需有效的方法来增强模型可信度。
AI.method: 基于局部替代模型的方法，结合定量保真度验证，生成并比较不同点云可解释性方法的合理性。
AI.result: 新方法在点云分类任务中提供了更准确、语义连贯且适用性广的解释，代码已开源。

search_text: Visualizing Global Explanations of Point Cloud DNNs 可视化点云 DNN 的全局解释 In the field of autonomous driving and robotics, point clouds are showing their excellent real-time performance as raw data from most of the mainstream 3D sensors. Therefore, point cloud neural networks have become a popular research direction in recent years. So far, however, there has been little discussion about the explainability of deep neural networks for point clouds. In this paper, we propose a point cloud-applicable explainability approach based on a local surrogate model-based method to show which components contribute to the classification. Moreover, we propose quantitative fidelity validations for generated explanations that enhance the persuasive power of explainability and compare the plausibility of different existing point cloud-applicable explainability methods. Our new explainability approach provides a fairly accurate, more semantically coherent and widely applicable explanation for point cloud classification tasks. Our code is available at https://github.com/Explain3D/LIME-3D 在自动驾驶和机器人领域，点云作为大多数主流3D传感器的原始数据，正在展示其出色的实时性能。因此，点云神经网络成为近年来的热门研究方向。然而，到目前为止，关于点云深度神经网络的可解释性的讨论还很少。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于局部代理模型的点云适用的可解释性方法，以显示哪些组件有助于分类。此外，我们提出对生成的解释进行定量保真度验证，以增强可解释性的说服力，并比较不同现有点云适用的可解释性方法的合理性。我们新的可解释性方法为点云分类任务提供了相当准确、语义更连贯且广泛适用的解释。我们的代码位于 https://github.com/Explain3D/LIME-3D 本文提出了一种适用于点云深度神经网络的可解释性方法，用于可视化其分类决策的全局解释。 Hanxiao Tan cs.CV

authors_text: Patrick M. Pilarski,Andrew Butcher,Elnaz Davoodi,Michael Bradley Johanson,Dylan J. A. Brenneis,Adam S. R. Parker,Leslie Acker,Matthew M. Botvinick,Joseph Modayil,Adam White
title: The Frost Hollow Experiments: Pavlovian Signalling as a Path to Coordination and Communication Between Agents
title_zh: Frost Hollow 实验：巴甫洛夫信号作为智能体之间协调和通信的途径
abstract: Learned communication between agents is a powerful tool when approaching decision-making problems that are hard to overcome by any single agent in isolation. However, continual coordination and communication learning between machine agents or human-machine partnerships remains a challenging open problem. As a stepping stone toward solving the continual communication learning problem, in this paper we contribute a multi-faceted study into what we term Pavlovian signalling -- a process by which learned, temporally extended predictions made by one agent inform decision-making by another agent with different perceptual access to their shared environment. We seek to establish how different temporal processes and representational choices impact Pavlovian signalling between learning agents. To do so, we introduce a partially observable decision-making domain we call the Frost Hollow. In this domain a prediction learning agent and a reinforcement learning agent are coupled into a two-part decision-making system that seeks to acquire sparse reward while avoiding time-conditional hazards. We evaluate two domain variations: 1) machine prediction and control learning in a linear walk, and 2) a prediction learning machine interacting with a human participant in a virtual reality environment. Our results showcase the speed of learning for Pavlovian signalling, the impact that different temporal representations do (and do not) have on agent-agent coordination, and how temporal aliasing impacts agent-agent and human-agent interactions differently. As a main contribution, we establish Pavlovian signalling as a natural bridge between fixed signalling paradigms and fully adaptive communication learning. Our results therefore point to an actionable, constructivist path towards continual communication learning between reinforcement learning agents, with potential impact in a range of real-world settings.

We seek to establish how different temporal processes and representational choices impact Pavlovian signalling between learning agents. To do so, we introduce a partially observable decision-making domain we call the Frost Hollow. Our results showcase the speed of learning for Pavlovian signalling, the impact that different temporal representations do (and do not) have on agent-agent coordination, and how temporal aliasing impacts agent-agent and human-agent interactions differently. As a main contribution, we establish Pavlovian signalling as a natural bridge between fixed signalling paradigms and fully adaptive communication learning. abstract_zh: 当解决任何单个智能体单独难以克服的决策问题时，智能体之间的学习交流是一个强大的工具。然而，机器代理或人机伙伴关系之间的持续协调和通信学习仍然是一个具有挑战性的开放问题。作为解决持续沟通学习问题的垫脚石，在本文中，我们对巴甫洛夫信号进行了多方面的研究，这是一个过程，一个智能体做出的学习的、时间上扩展的预测为另一个对共享环境有不同感知访问的智能体的决策提供信息。我们试图确定不同的时间过程和表征选择如何影响学习代理之间的巴甫洛夫信号。为此，我们引入了一个部分可观察的决策域，我们称之为“Frost Hollow”。在这个领域中，预测学习代理和强化学习代理耦合成一个由两部分组成的决策系统，该系统寻求获得稀疏奖励，同时避免时间条件风险。我们评估两个领域的变化：1）线性行走中的机器预测和控制学习，2）预测学习机在虚拟现实环境中与人类参与者交互。我们的结果展示了巴甫洛夫信号的学习速度、不同时间表示对智能体-智能体协调的影响（和不影响），以及时间混叠如何以不同的方式影响智能体-智能体和人类-智能体交互。作为主要贡献，我们将巴甫洛夫信号建立为固定信号范式和完全自适应通信学习之间的天然桥梁。因此，我们的结果指出了强化学习代理之间持续沟通学习的可行的、建构主义的路径，对一系列现实世界环境具有潜在影响。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过研究'巴甫洛夫信号'机制，探索学习代理间协调与通信的连续学习问题，设计了部分可观测决策域'Frost Hollow'进行实验验证。
AI.motivation: 解决机器代理或人机协作中持续协调与通信学习的挑战，建立固定信号范式与完全自适应通信学习之间的桥梁。
AI.method: 构建Frost Hollow实验环境，包含预测学习代理与强化学习代理的双代理系统，通过线性行走任务和虚拟现实环境中的人机交互任务进行对比实验。
AI.result: 验证了巴甫洛夫信号的学习速度，揭示了不同时间表示对代理协调的影响差异，发现时间混淆对人机交互和代理交互的影响存在本质区别。
AI.conclusion: 确立巴甫洛夫信号作为连接固定信号范式与自适应通信学习的自然桥梁，为多智能体连续通信学习提供了可操作的建构主义路径。

authors_text: Charlie Nash,João Carreira,Jacob Walker,Iain Barr,Andrew Jaegle,Mateusz Malinowski,Peter Battaglia
title: Transframer: Arbitrary Frame Prediction with Generative Models
title_zh: Transframer：使用生成模型进行任意帧预测
abstract: We present a general-purpose framework for image modelling and vision tasks based on probabilistic frame prediction. Our approach unifies a broad range of tasks, from image segmentation, to novel view synthesis and video interpolation. We pair this framework with an architecture we term Transframer, which uses U-Net and Transformer components to condition on annotated context frames, and outputs sequences of sparse, compressed image features. Transframer is the state-of-the-art on a variety of video generation benchmarks, is competitive with the strongest models on few-shot view synthesis, and can generate coherent 30 second videos from a single image without any explicit geometric information. A single generalist Transframer simultaneously produces promising results on 8 tasks, including semantic segmentation, image classification and optical flow prediction with no task-specific architectural components, demonstrating that multi-task computer vision can be tackled using probabilistic image models. Our approach can in principle be applied to a wide range of applications that require learning the conditional structure of annotated image-formatted data.
summary: We present a general-purpose framework for image modelling and vision tasks based on probabilistic frame prediction. Our approach unifies a broad range of tasks, from image segmentation, to novel view synthesis and video interpolation. We pair this framework with an architecture we term Transframer, which uses U-Net and Transformer components to condition on annotated context frames, and outputs sequences of sparse, compressed image features. Transframer is the state-of-the-art on a variety of video generation benchmarks, is competitive with the strongest models on few-shot view synthesis, and can generate coherent 30 second videos from a single image without any explicit geometric information. A single generalist Transframer simultaneously produces promising results on 8 tasks, including semantic segmentation, image classification and optical flow prediction with no task-specific architectural components, demonstrating that multi-task computer vision can be tackled using probabilistic image models. Our approach can in principle be applied to a wide range of applications that require learning the conditional structure of annotated image-formatted data.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个基于概率帧预测的图像建模和视觉任务的通用框架。我们的方法统一了广泛的任务，从图像分割到新颖的视图合成和视频插值。我们将此框架与我们称为 Transframer 的架构配对，该架构使用 U-Net 和 Transformer 组件来调节带注释的上下文帧，并输出稀疏、压缩的图像特征序列。 Transframer 在各种视频生成基准上都是最先进的，在少镜头视图合成方面与最强的模型具有竞争力，并且可以从单个图像生成连贯的 30 秒视频，而无需任何显式几何信息。单个多面手 Transframer 可同时在 8 个任务上产生有希望的结果，包括语义分割、图像分类和光流预测，而无需特定于任务的架构组件，这表明可以使用概率图像模型来处理多任务计算机视觉。我们的方法原则上可以应用于需要学习带注释的图像格式数据的条件结构的广泛应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于概率帧预测的通用框架Transframer，用于解决图像分割、视角合成、视频插值等多类视觉任务。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉模型通常针对单一任务设计，缺乏通用性。本文旨在通过概率图像模型统一处理多种视觉任务，提升模型的泛化能力与效率。
AI.method: 设计Transframer架构，结合U-Net与Transformer组件，利用标注上下文帧生成稀疏压缩图像特征，无需任务特定模块即可处理多类任务。
AI.result: Transframer在视频生成基准中达到SOTA，在少样本视角合成和单图生成30秒视频任务中表现优异，同时在8个任务（如分割、分类）中均取得有竞争力的结果。
AI.conclusion: 概率图像模型可有效支撑多任务视觉学习，Transframer框架展示了通用模型在复杂视觉任务中的潜力与应用价值。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于概率帧预测的通用框架Transframer，用于解决图像分割、视角合成、视频插值等多类视觉任务。

search_text: Transframer: Arbitrary Frame Prediction with Generative Models Transframer：使用生成模型进行任意帧预测 We present a general-purpose framework for image modelling and vision tasks based on probabilistic frame prediction. Our approach unifies a broad range of tasks, from image segmentation, to novel view synthesis and video interpolation. We pair this framework with an architecture we term Transframer, which uses U-Net and Transformer components to condition on annotated context frames, and outputs sequences of sparse, compressed image features. Transframer is the state-of-the-art on a variety of video generation benchmarks, is competitive with the strongest models on few-shot view synthesis, and can generate coherent 30 second videos from a single image without any explicit geometric information. A single generalist Transframer simultaneously produces promising results on 8 tasks, including semantic segmentation, image classification and optical flow prediction with no task-specific architectural components, demonstrating that multi-task computer vision can be tackled using probabilistic image models. Our approach can in principle be applied to a wide range of applications that require learning the conditional structure of annotated image-formatted data. 我们提出了一个基于概率帧预测的图像建模和视觉任务的通用框架。我们的方法统一了广泛的任务，从图像分割到新颖的视图合成和视频插值。我们将此框架与我们称为 Transframer 的架构配对，该架构使用 U-Net 和 Transformer 组件来调节带注释的上下文帧，并输出稀疏、压缩的图像特征序列。 Transframer 在各种视频生成基准上都是最先进的，在少镜头视图合成方面与最强的模型具有竞争力，并且可以从单个图像生成连贯的 30 秒视频，而无需任何显式几何信息。单个多面手 Transframer 可同时在 8 个任务上产生有希望的结果，包括语义分割、图像分类和光流预测，而无需特定于任务的架构组件，这表明可以使用概率图像模型来处理多任务计算机视觉。我们的方法原则上可以应用于需要学习带注释的图像格式数据的条件结构的广泛应用。 本文提出一种基于概率帧预测的通用框架Transframer，用于解决图像分割、视角合成、视频插值等多类视觉任务。 Charlie Nash,João Carreira,Jacob Walker,Iain Barr,Andrew Jaegle,Mateusz Malinowski,Peter Battaglia cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Ruihan Yang,Prakhar Srivastava,Stephan Mandt
title: Diffusion Probabilistic Modeling for Video Generation
title_zh: 用于视频生成的扩散概率建模
abstract: Denoising diffusion probabilistic models are a promising new class of generative models that mark a milestone in high-quality image generation. This paper showcases their ability to sequentially generate video, surpassing prior methods in perceptual and probabilistic forecasting metrics. We propose an autoregressive, end-to-end optimized video diffusion model inspired by recent advances in neural video compression. The model successively generates future frames by correcting a deterministic next-frame prediction using a stochastic residual generated by an inverse diffusion process. We compare this approach against five baselines on four datasets involving natural and simulation-based videos. We find significant improvements in terms of perceptual quality for all datasets. Furthermore, by introducing a scalable version of the Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) applicable to video, we show that our model also outperforms existing approaches in their probabilistic frame forecasting ability.
summary: Denoising diffusion probabilistic models are a promising new class of generative models that mark a milestone in high-quality image generation. This paper showcases their ability to sequentially generate video, surpassing prior methods in perceptual and probabilistic forecasting metrics. We propose an autoregressive, end-to-end optimized video diffusion model inspired by recent advances in neural video compression. The model successively generates future frames by correcting a deterministic next-frame prediction using a stochastic residual generated by an inverse diffusion process. We compare this approach against five baselines on four datasets involving natural and simulation-based videos. We find significant improvements in terms of perceptual quality for all datasets. Furthermore, by introducing a scalable version of the Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) applicable to video, we show that our model also outperforms existing approaches in their probabilistic frame forecasting ability.
abstract_zh: 去噪扩散概率模型是一类有前途的新型生成模型，标志着高质量图像生成的里程碑。本文展示了他们顺序生成视频的能力，在感知和概率预测指标方面超越了先前的方法。受神经视频压缩最新进展的启发，我们提出了一种自回归、端到端优化的视频扩散模型。该模型通过使用逆扩散过程生成的随机残差校正确定性下一帧预测来连续生成未来帧。我们将这种方法与涉及自然和模拟视频的四个数据集的五个基线进行比较。我们发现所有数据集的感知质量都有显着改善。此外，通过引入适用于视频的连续排名概率得分（CRPS）的可扩展版本，我们表明我们的模型在概率帧预测能力方面也优于现有方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于扩散概率模型的视频生成方法，通过自回归和端到端优化生成高质量视频。
AI.motivation: 为了解决现有视频生成方法在感知质量与概率预测方面的不足，本文引入扩散概率模型以提升生成效果和预测准确性。
AI.method: 提出了一种受神经视频压缩启发的自回归端到端优化模型，通过逆扩散过程生成随机残差修正确定性帧预测。
AI.result: 在四个自然和模拟视频数据集上，感知质量显著提升，并通过改进的CRPS指标证明了概率预测能力的优越性。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的模型在视频生成质量和概率预测上均优于现有方法，为视频生成领域提供了新思路。

authors_text: Akshansh Mishra,Asmita Suman,Devarrishi Dixit
title: Computer Vision Algorithm for Predicting the Welding Efficiency of Friction Stir Welded Copper Joints from its Microstructures
title_zh: 从微观结构预测搅拌摩擦焊铜接头焊接效率的计算机视觉算法
abstract: Friction Stir Welding is a robust joining process, and numerous AI-based algorithms are being developed in this field to enhance mechanical and microstructure properties. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are Artificial Neural Networks that use image data as input. Identical to Artificial Neural Networks, they are composed of weights that are determined throughout learning, neurons (activated functions), and a goal (loss function). CNN is utilized in a variety of applications, including image recognition, semantic segmentation, image recognition, and localization. Utilizing training on 3000 microstructure pictures and new tests on 300 microstructure photographs, the current work investigates the predictions of Friction Stir Welded joint effectiveness using microstructure images.
summary: Friction Stir Welding is a robust joining process, and numerous AI-based algorithms are being developed in this field to enhance mechanical and microstructure properties. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are Artificial Neural Networks that use image data as input. Identical to Artificial Neural Networks, they are composed of weights that are determined throughout learning, neurons (activated functions), and a goal (loss function). CNN is utilized in a variety of applications, including image recognition, semantic segmentation, image recognition, and localization. Utilizing training on 3000 microstructure pictures and new tests on 300 microstructure photographs, the current work investigates the predictions of Friction Stir Welded joint effectiveness using microstructure images.
abstract_zh: 搅拌摩擦焊是一种稳健的连接工艺，该领域正在开发许多基于人工智能的算法，以增强机械和微观结构性能。卷积神经网络 (CNN) 是使用图像数据作为输入的人工神经网络。与人工神经网络相同，它们由在学习过程中确定的权重、神经元（激活函数）和目标（损失函数）组成。 CNN 可用于多种应用，包括图像识别、语义分割、图像识别和定位。通过对 3000 张微观结构图片进行训练以及对 300 张微观结构照片进行新测试，当前的工作研究了使用微观结构图像预测搅拌摩擦焊接头的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的计算机视觉算法，通过分析摩擦搅拌焊接铜接头的微观结构图像预测焊接效率。
AI.motivation: 摩擦搅拌焊接的机械性能与微观结构密切相关，但传统方法难以高效量化预测焊接效率，亟需开发基于AI的自动化分析工具。
AI.method: 采用卷积神经网络模型，使用3000张微观结构图像进行训练，并通过300张独立测试图像验证模型对焊接效率的预测能力。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该算法能够有效从微观结构特征中学习焊接效率的预测模式，验证了深度学习在材料性能评估中的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 基于CNN的计算机视觉方法可为摩擦搅拌焊接质量控制提供快速、非破坏性的微观结构分析手段，具有工程应用潜力。

authors_text: Ruilin Li,Ruobin Gao,P. N. Suganthan
title: A Decomposition-Based Hybrid Ensemble CNN Framework for Driver Fatigue Recognition
title_zh: 基于分解的混合集成 CNN 驾驶员疲劳识别框架
abstract: Electroencephalogram (EEG) has become increasingly popular in driver fatigue monitoring systems. Several decomposition methods have been attempted to analyze the EEG signals that are complex, nonlinear and non-stationary and improve the EEG decoding performance in different applications. However, it remains challenging to extract more distinguishable features from different decomposed components for driver fatigue recognition. In this work, we propose a novel decomposition-based hybrid ensemble convolutional neural network (CNN) framework to enhance the capability of decoding EEG signals. Four decomposition methods are employed to disassemble the EEG signals into components of different complexity. Instead of handcraft features, the CNNs in this framework directly learn from the decomposed components. In addition, a component-specific batch normalization layer is employed to reduce subject variability. Moreover, we employ two ensemble modes to integrate the outputs of all CNNs, comprehensively exploiting the diverse information of the decomposed components. Against the challenging cross-subject driver fatigue recognition task, the models under the framework all showed superior performance to the strong baselines. Specifically, the performance of different decomposition methods and ensemble modes was further compared. The results indicated that discrete wavelet transform-based ensemble CNN achieved the highest average classification accuracy of 83.48% among the compared methods. The proposed framework can be extended to any CNN architecture and be applied to any EEG-related tasks, opening the possibility of extracting more beneficial features from complex EEG data.
summary: Electroencephalogram (EEG) has become increasingly popular in driver fatigue monitoring systems. Several decomposition methods have been attempted to analyze the EEG signals that are complex, nonlinear and non-stationary and improve the EEG decoding performance in different applications. However, it remains challenging to extract more distinguishable features from different decomposed components for driver fatigue recognition. In this work, we propose a novel decomposition-based hybrid ensemble convolutional neural network (CNN) framework to enhance the capability of decoding EEG signals. Four decomposition methods are employed to disassemble the EEG signals into components of different complexity. Instead of handcraft features, the CNNs in this framework directly learn from the decomposed components. In addition, a component-specific batch normalization layer is employed to reduce subject variability. Moreover, we employ two ensemble modes to integrate the outputs of all CNNs, comprehensively exploiting the diverse information of the decomposed components. Against the challenging cross-subject driver fatigue recognition task, the models under the framework all showed superior performance to the strong baselines. Specifically, the performance of different decomposition methods and ensemble modes was further compared. The results indicated that discrete wavelet transform-based ensemble CNN achieved the highest average classification accuracy of 83.48% among the compared methods. The proposed framework can be extended to any CNN architecture and be applied to any EEG-related tasks, opening the possibility of extracting more beneficial features from complex EEG data.
abstract_zh: 脑电图 (EEG) 在驾驶员疲劳监测系统中越来越受欢迎。人们尝试了几种分解方法来分析复杂、非线性和非平稳的脑电信号，并提高不同应用中的脑电解码性能。然而，从不同的分解成分中提取更多可区分的特征来进行驾驶员疲劳识别仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于分解的混合集成卷积神经网络（CNN）框架来增强解码脑电图信号的能力。采用四种分解方法将脑电图信号分解成不同复杂度的分量。该框架中的 CNN 不是手工制作特征，而是直接从分解的组件中学习。此外，还采用特定于组件的批量归一化层来减少受试者的变异性。此外，我们采用两种集成模式来集成所有 CNN 的输出，综合利用分解组件的不同信息。针对具有挑战性的跨学科驾驶员疲劳识别任务，该框架下的模型均表现出优于强基线的性能。具体来说，进一步比较了不同分解方法和集成模式的性能。结果表明，在比较方法中，基于离散小波变换的集成 CNN 实现了最高的平均分类精度，达到 83.48%。所提出的框架可以扩展到任何 CNN 架构，并应用于任何脑电图相关任务，从而开启了从复杂脑电图数据中提取更多有用特征的可能性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于分解的混合集成卷积神经网络（CNN）框架，用于提升驾驶员疲劳识别中的脑电信号（EEG）解码能力。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以从复杂分解的EEG成分中提取可区分特征，且传统手工特征限制了模型性能，需探索更有效的分解与深度学习结合方案。
AI.method: 采用四种分解方法拆分EEG信号，利用CNN直接学习分解成分；引入组件特定的批量归一化层减少个体差异，并通过两种集成模式融合多CNN输出。
AI.result: 在跨被试疲劳识别任务中，框架性能优于强基线，其中小波变换集成CNN达到83.48%的最高分类准确率。
AI.conclusion: 该框架可扩展至任意CNN架构及EEG相关任务，为复杂EEG数据的特征提取提供了新途径。

search_text: A Decomposition-Based Hybrid Ensemble CNN Framework for Driver Fatigue Recognition 基于分解的混合集成 CNN 驾驶员疲劳识别框架 Electroencephalogram (EEG) has become increasingly popular in driver fatigue monitoring systems. Several decomposition methods have been attempted to analyze the EEG signals that are complex, nonlinear and non-stationary and improve the EEG decoding performance in different applications. However, it remains challenging to extract more distinguishable features from different decomposed components for driver fatigue recognition. In this work, we propose a novel decomposition-based hybrid ensemble convolutional neural network (CNN) framework to enhance the capability of decoding EEG signals. Four decomposition methods are employed to disassemble the EEG signals into components of different complexity. Instead of handcraft features, the CNNs in this framework directly learn from the decomposed components. In addition, a component-specific batch normalization layer is employed to reduce subject variability. Moreover, we employ two ensemble modes to integrate the outputs of all CNNs, comprehensively exploiting the diverse information of the decomposed components. Against the challenging cross-subject driver fatigue recognition task, the models under the framework all showed superior performance to the strong baselines. Specifically, the performance of different decomposition methods and ensemble modes was further compared. The results indicated that discrete wavelet transform-based ensemble CNN achieved the highest average classification accuracy of 83.48% among the compared methods. The proposed framework can be extended to any CNN architecture and be applied to any EEG-related tasks, opening the possibility of extracting more beneficial features from complex EEG data. 脑电图 (EEG) 在驾驶员疲劳监测系统中越来越受欢迎。人们尝试了几种分解方法来分析复杂、非线性和非平稳的脑电信号，并提高不同应用中的脑电解码性能。然而，从不同的分解成分中提取更多可区分的特征来进行驾驶员疲劳识别仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于分解的混合集成卷积神经网络（CNN）框架来增强解码脑电图信号的能力。采用四种分解方法将脑电图信号分解成不同复杂度的分量。该框架中的 CNN 不是手工制作特征，而是直接从分解的组件中学习。此外，还采用特定于组件的批量归一化层来减少受试者的变异性。此外，我们采用两种集成模式来集成所有 CNN 的输出，综合利用分解组件的不同信息。针对具有挑战性的跨学科驾驶员疲劳识别任务，该框架下的模型均表现出优于强基线的性能。具体来说，进一步比较了不同分解方法和集成模式的性能。结果表明，在比较方法中，基于离散小波变换的集成 CNN 实现了最高的平均分类精度，达到 83.48%。所提出的框架可以扩展到任何 CNN 架构，并应用于任何脑电图相关任务，从而开启了从复杂脑电图数据中提取更多有用特征的可能性。 本文提出了一种基于分解的混合集成卷积神经网络（CNN）框架，用于提升驾驶员疲劳识别中的脑电信号（EEG）解码能力。 Ruilin Li,Ruobin Gao,P. authors_text: Mor Sinay,Noa Agmon,Oleg Maksimov,Aviad Fux,Sarit Kraus
title: Uncertainty with UAV Search of Multiple Goal-oriented Targets
title_zh: 无人机搜索多个目标的不确定性
abstract: This paper considers the complex problem of a team of UAVs searching targets under uncertainty. The goal of the UAV team is to find all of the moving targets as quickly as possible before they arrive at their selected goal. The uncertainty considered is threefold: First, the UAVs do not know the targets' locations and destinations. Second, the sensing capabilities of the UAVs are not perfect. Third, the targets' movement model is unknown. We suggest a real-time algorithmic framework for the UAVs, combining entropy and stochastic-temporal belief, that aims at optimizing the probability of a quick and successful detection of all of the targets. We have empirically evaluated the algorithmic framework, and have shown its efficiency and significant performance improvement compared to other solutions. Furthermore, we have evaluated our framework using Peer Designed Agents (PDAs), which are computer agents that simulate targets and show that our algorithmic framework outperforms other solutions in this scenario.
summary: This paper considers the complex problem of a team of UAVs searching targets under uncertainty. The goal of the UAV team is to find all of the moving targets as quickly as possible before they arrive at their selected goal. The uncertainty considered is threefold: First, the UAVs do not know the targets' locations and destinations. Second, the sensing capabilities of the UAVs are not perfect. Third, the targets' movement model is unknown. We suggest a real-time algorithmic framework for the UAVs, combining entropy and stochastic-temporal belief, that aims at optimizing the probability of a quick and successful detection of all of the targets. We have empirically evaluated the algorithmic framework, and have shown its efficiency and significant performance improvement compared to other solutions. Furthermore, we have evaluated our framework using Peer Designed Agents (PDAs), which are computer agents that simulate targets and show that our algorithmic framework outperforms other solutions in this scenario.
abstract_zh: 本文考虑了一组无人机在不确定情况下搜索目标的复杂问题。无人机团队的目标是在所有移动目标到达选定目标之前尽快找到它们。考虑的不确定性有三个：首先，无人机不知道目标的位置和目的地。其次，无人机的感知能力并不完善。第三，目标的运动模型未知。我们提出了一种无人机实时算法框架，结合了熵和随机时间信念，旨在优化快速成功检测所有目标的概率。我们对算法框架进行了实证评估，并显示了与其他解决方案相比其效率和显着的性能改进。此外，我们还使用同行设计代理（PDA）评估了我们的框架，PDA 是模拟目标的计算机代理，并表明我们的算法框架在此场景中优于其他解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文主要研究在不确定环境下，无人机团队如何高效搜索多个移动目标，以尽快找到所有目标并阻止其到达目的地。
AI.motivation: 由于目标位置、目的地未知，感知能力有限以及移动模型不确定，传统方法难以有效应对，因此需要一种新的算法框架来提高搜索效率。
AI.method: 提出结合熵与随机时间信念的实时算法框架，优化无人机搜索策略，以提高检测所有目标的概率。
AI.result: 实证评估和PDAs测试显示，该框架相比其他方法在搜索效率和成功率上显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的算法框架有效应对多源不确定性，在无人机搜索任务中表现优于现有方法。

authors_text: Hao Ding,Jintan Zhang,Peter Kazanzides,Jie Ying Wu,Mathias Unberath
title: CaRTS: Causality-driven Robot Tool Segmentation from Vision and Kinematics Data
title_zh: CaRTS：根据视觉和运动学数据进行因果驱动的机器人工具分割
abstract: Vision-based segmentation of the robotic tool during robot-assisted surgery enables downstream applications, such as augmented reality feedback, while allowing for inaccuracies in robot kinematics. With the introduction of deep learning, many methods were presented to solve instrument segmentation directly and solely from images. While these approaches made remarkable progress on benchmark datasets, fundamental challenges pertaining to their robustness remain. We present CaRTS, a causality-driven robot tool segmentation algorithm, that is designed based on a complementary causal model of the robot tool segmentation task. Rather than directly inferring segmentation masks from observed images, CaRTS iteratively aligns tool models with image observations by updating the initially incorrect robot kinematic parameters through forward kinematics and differentiable rendering to optimize image feature similarity end-to-end. We benchmark CaRTS with competing techniques on both synthetic as well as real data from the dVRK, generated in precisely controlled scenarios to allow for counterfactual synthesis. On training-domain test data, CaRTS achieves a Dice score of 93.4 that is preserved well (Dice score of 91.8) when tested on counterfactually altered test data, exhibiting low brightness, smoke, blood, and altered background patterns. This compares favorably to Dice scores of 95.0 and 86.7, respectively, of the SOTA image-based method. Future work will involve accelerating CaRTS to achieve video framerate and estimating the impact occlusion has in practice. Despite these limitations, our results are promising: In addition to achieving high segmentation accuracy, CaRTS provides estimates of the true robot kinematics, which may benefit applications such as force estimation. Code is available at: https://github.com/hding2455/CaRTS
summary: Vision-based segmentation of the robotic tool during robot-assisted surgery enables downstream applications, such as augmented reality feedback, while allowing for inaccuracies in robot kinematics. With the introduction of deep learning, many methods were presented to solve instrument segmentation directly and solely from images. While these approaches made remarkable progress on benchmark datasets, fundamental challenges pertaining to their robustness remain. We present CaRTS, a causality-driven robot tool segmentation algorithm, that is designed based on a complementary causal model of the robot tool segmentation task. Rather than directly inferring segmentation masks from observed images, CaRTS iteratively aligns tool models with image observations by updating the initially incorrect robot kinematic parameters through forward kinematics and differentiable rendering to optimize image feature similarity end-to-end. We benchmark CaRTS with competing techniques on both synthetic as well as real data from the dVRK, generated in precisely controlled scenarios to allow for counterfactual synthesis. On training-domain test data, CaRTS achieves a Dice score of 93.4 that is preserved well (Dice score of 91.8) when tested on counterfactually altered test data, exhibiting low brightness, smoke, blood, and altered background patterns. This compares favorably to Dice scores of 95.0 and 86.7, respectively, of the SOTA image-based method. Future work will involve accelerating CaRTS to achieve video framerate and estimating the impact occlusion has in practice. Despite these limitations, our results are promising: In addition to achieving high segmentation accuracy, CaRTS provides estimates of the true robot kinematics, which may benefit applications such as force estimation. Code is available at: https://github.com/hding2455/CaRTS
abstract_zh: 在机器人辅助手术过程中，基于视觉的机器人工具分割可以实现下游应用，例如增强现实反馈，同时允许机器人运动学的不准确。随着深度学习的引入，出现了许多方法来直接从图像中解决仪器分割问题。尽管这些方法在基准数据集上取得了显着进展，但与其稳健性相关的根本挑战仍然存在。我们提出了 CaRTS，一种因果驱动的机器人工具分割算法，它是基于机器人工具分割任务的互补因果模型设计的。 CaRTS 不是直接从观察到的图像推断分割掩模，而是通过正向运动学和可微渲染更新最初不正确的机器人运动学参数，迭代地将工具模型与图像观察对齐，以优化端到端的图像特征相似性。我们使用来自 dVRK 的合成数据和真实数据的竞争技术对 CaRTS 进行基准测试，这些数据是在精确控制的场景中生成的，以允许反事实合成。在训练域测试数据上，CaRTS 的 Dice 分数为 93.4，当对反事实改变的测试数据进行测试时，该分数保持良好（Dice 分数为 91.8），表现出低亮度、烟雾、血液和改变的背景图案。相比之下，基于 SOTA 图像的方法的 Dice 分数分别为 95.0 和 86.7。未来的工作将涉及加速 CaRTS 以实现视频帧速率并估计遮挡在实践中的影响。尽管存在这些限制，我们的结果还是有希望的：除了实现高分割精度之外，CaRTS 还提供了真实机器人运动学的估计，这可能有利于力估计等应用。代码位于：https://github.com/hding2455/CaRTS

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于因果关系的机器人工具分割算法CaRTS，通过结合视觉和运动学数据提高手术中机器人工具分割的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有基于图像的分割方法在光照、烟雾等真实场景干扰下表现不稳定，而融合运动学信息可提升分割可靠性。
AI.method: 构建互补因果模型，通过正向运动学和可微渲染迭代优化初始错误的运动学参数，使工具模型与图像观测对齐。
AI.result: 在合成及真实dVRK数据集上，CaRTS在训练域测试得分为93.4，对抗性测试保持91.8，优于SOTA图像方法的86.7。
AI.conclusion: CaRTS实现了高精度分割并估计真实运动学参数，为力估计等应用提供支持，后续需优化实时性和遮挡处理。

search_text: CaRTS: Causality-driven Robot Tool Segmentation from Vision and Kinematics Data CaRTS：根据视觉和运动学数据进行因果驱动的机器人工具分割 Vision-based segmentation of the robotic tool during robot-assisted surgery enables downstream applications, such as augmented reality feedback, while allowing for inaccuracies in robot kinematics. With the introduction of deep learning, many methods were presented to solve instrument segmentation directly and solely from images. While these approaches made remarkable progress on benchmark datasets, fundamental challenges pertaining to their robustness remain. We present CaRTS, a causality-driven robot tool segmentation algorithm, that is designed based on a complementary causal model of the robot tool segmentation task. Rather than directly inferring segmentation masks from observed images, CaRTS iteratively aligns tool models with image observations by updating the initially incorrect robot kinematic parameters through forward kinematics and differentiable rendering to optimize image feature similarity end-to-end. We benchmark CaRTS with competing techniques on both synthetic as well as real data from the dVRK, generated in precisely controlled scenarios to allow for counterfactual synthesis. On training-domain test data, CaRTS achieves a Dice score of 93.4 that is preserved well (Dice score of 91.8) when tested on counterfactually altered test data, exhibiting low brightness, smoke, blood, and altered background patterns. This compares favorably to Dice scores of 95.0 and 86.7, respectively, of the SOTA image-based method. Future work will involve accelerating CaRTS to achieve video framerate and estimating the impact occlusion has in practice. Despite these limitations, our results are promising: In addition to achieving high segmentation accuracy, CaRTS provides estimates of the true robot kinematics, which may benefit applications such as force estimation. Code is available at: https://github.com/hding2455/CaRTS 在机器人辅助手术过程中，基于视觉的机器人工具分割可以实现下游应用，例如增强现实反馈，同时允许机器人运动学的不准确。随着深度学习的引入，出现了许多方法来直接从图像中解决仪器分割问题。尽管这些方法在基准数据集上取得了显着进展，但与其稳健性相关的根本挑战仍然存在。我们提出了 CaRTS，一种因果驱动的机器人工具分割算法，它是基于机器人工具分割任务的互补因果模型设计的。 CaRTS 不是直接从观察到的图像推断分割掩模，而是通过正向运动学和可微渲染更新最初不正确的机器人运动学参数，迭代地将工具模型与图像观察对齐，以优化端到端的图像特征相似性。我们使用来自 dVRK 的合成数据和真实数据的竞争技术对 CaRTS 进行基准测试，这些数据是在精确控制的场景中生成的，以允许反事实合成。在训练域测试数据上，CaRTS 的 Dice 分数为 93.4，当对反事实改变的测试数据进行测试时，该分数保持良好（Dice 分数为 91.8），表现出低亮度、烟雾、血液和改变的背景图案。相比之下，基于 SOTA 图像的方法的 Dice 分数分别为 95.0 和 86.7。未来的工作将涉及加速 CaRTS 以实现视频帧速率并估计遮挡在实践中的影响。尽管存在这些限制，我们的结果还是有希望的：除了实现高分割精度之外，CaRTS 还提供了真实机器人运动学的估计，这可能有利于力估计等应用。代码位于：https://github.com/hding2455/CaRTS 本文提出了一种基于因果关系的机器人工具分割算法CaRTS，通过结合视觉和运动学数据提高手术中机器人工具分割的鲁棒性。 Hao Ding,Jintan Zhang,Peter Kazanzides,Jie Ying Wu,Mathias Unberath cs.RO,cs.CV

authors_text: Krzysztof M. Graczyk,Jaroslaw Pawlowski,Sylwia Majchrowska,Tomasz Golan
title: Self-Normalized Density Map (SNDM) for Counting Microbiological Objects
title_zh: 用于计数微生物对象的自归一化密度图 (SNDM)
abstract: The statistical properties of the density map (DM) approach to counting microbiological objects on images are studied in detail. The DM is given by U$^2$-Net. Two statistical methods for deep neural networks are utilized: the bootstrap and the Monte Carlo (MC) dropout. The detailed analysis of the uncertainties for the DM predictions leads to a deeper understanding of the DM model's deficiencies. Based on our investigation, we propose a self-normalization module in the network. The improved network model, called \textit{Self-Normalized Density Map} (SNDM), can correct its output density map by itself to accurately predict the total number of objects in the image. The SNDM architecture outperforms the original model. Moreover, both statistical frameworks -- bootstrap and MC dropout -- have consistent statistical results for SNDM, which were not observed in the original model. The SNDM efficiency is comparable with the detector-base models, such as Faster and Cascade R-CNN detectors.
summary: The statistical properties of the density map (DM) approach to counting microbiological objects on images are studied in detail. The DM is given by U$^2$-Net. Two statistical methods for deep neural networks are utilized: the bootstrap and the Monte Carlo (MC) dropout. The detailed analysis of the uncertainties for the DM predictions leads to a deeper understanding of the DM model's deficiencies. Based on our investigation, we propose a self-normalization module in the network. The improved network model, called \textit{Self-Normalized Density Map} (SNDM), can correct its output density map by itself to accurately predict the total number of objects in the image. The SNDM architecture outperforms the original model. Moreover, both statistical frameworks -- bootstrap and MC dropout -- have consistent statistical results for SNDM, which were not observed in the original model. The SNDM efficiency is comparable with the detector-base models, such as Faster and Cascade R-CNN detectors.
abstract_zh: 详细研究了用于计数图像上微生物对象的密度图（DM）方法的统计特性。 DM 由 U$^2$-Net 给出。使用深度神经网络的两种统计方法：引导程序和蒙特卡罗（MC）丢失。对 DM 预测不确定性的详细分析有助于更深入地了解 DM 模型的缺陷。根据我们的调查，我们提出了网络中的自标准化模块。改进的网络模型称为 \textit{自归一化密度图} (SNDM)，可以自行校正其输出密度图，以准确预测图像中的对象总数。 SNDM 架构优于原始模型。此外，两种统计框架（bootstrap 和 MC dropout）对于 SNDM 都有一致的统计结果，而这在原始模型中是没有观察到的。 SNDM 效率与基于检测器的模型相当，例如 Faster 和 Cascade R-CNN 检测器。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自归一化密度图（SNDM）方法，用于在图像中准确计数微生物对象，通过改进密度图模型的不确定性处理机制。
AI.motivation: 传统密度图方法（如U²-Net）在预测微生物对象数量时存在不确定性缺陷，导致计数不准确，需通过自归一化机制提升模型可靠性。
AI.method: 基于U²-Net框架，结合引导抽样和蒙特卡洛Dropout两种统计方法分析密度图预测的不确定性，并引入自归一化模块修正输出密度图。
AI.result: SNDM模型在计数精度上优于原始模型，两种统计方法在SNDM上得出一致结果，且效率与Faster R-CNN等检测器相当。
AI.conclusion: SNDM通过自归一化有效修正密度图，解决了原始模型的不确定性问题，为微生物对象计数提供了高效且可靠的解决方案。

authors_text: Yan Wang,Yixuan Sun,Yiwen Huang,Zhongying Liu,Shuyong Gao,Wei Zhang,Weifeng Ge,Wenqiang Zhang
title: FERV39k: A Large-Scale Multi-Scene Dataset for Facial Expression Recognition in Videos
title_zh: FERV39k：用于视频中面部表情识别的大规模多场景数据集
abstract: Current benchmarks for facial expression recognition (FER) mainly focus on static images, while there are limited datasets for FER in videos. It is still ambiguous to evaluate whether performances of existing methods remain satisfactory in real-world application-oriented scenes. For example, the "Happy" expression with high intensity in Talk-Show is more discriminating than the same expression with low intensity in Official-Event. To fill this gap, we build a large-scale multi-scene dataset, coined as FERV39k. We analyze the important ingredients of constructing such a novel dataset in three aspects: (1) multi-scene hierarchy and expression class, (2) generation of candidate video clips, (3) trusted manual labelling process. Based on these guidelines, we select 4 scenarios subdivided into 22 scenes, annotate 86k samples automatically obtained from 4k videos based on the well-designed workflow, and finally build 38,935 video clips labeled with 7 classic expressions. Experiment benchmarks on four kinds of baseline frameworks were also provided and further analysis on their performance across different scenes and some challenges for future research were given. Besides, we systematically investigate key components of DFER by ablation studies. The baseline framework and our project will be available.
summary: Current benchmarks for facial expression recognition (FER) mainly focus on static images, while there are limited datasets for FER in videos. It is still ambiguous to evaluate whether performances of existing methods remain satisfactory in real-world application-oriented scenes. For example, the "Happy" expression with high intensity in Talk-Show is more discriminating than the same expression with low intensity in Official-Event. To fill this gap, we build a large-scale multi-scene dataset, coined as FERV39k. We analyze the important ingredients of constructing such a novel dataset in three aspects: (1) multi-scene hierarchy and expression class, (2) generation of candidate video clips, (3) trusted manual labelling process. Based on these guidelines, we select 4 scenarios subdivided into 22 scenes, annotate 86k samples automatically obtained from 4k videos based on the well-designed workflow, and finally build 38,935 video clips labeled with 7 classic expressions. Experiment benchmarks on four kinds of baseline frameworks were also provided and further analysis on their performance across different scenes and some challenges for future research were given. Besides, we systematically investigate key components of DFER by ablation studies. The baseline framework and our project will be available.
abstract_zh: 目前面部表情识别（FER）的基准主要集中在静态图像，而视频中的 FER 数据集有限。评估现有方法的性能在现实世界的面向应用的场景中是否仍然令人满意仍然不明确。例如，脱口秀中高强度的“快乐”表达比官方活动中低强度的相同表达更具辨别力。为了填补这一空白，我们构建了一个大规模多场景数据集，命名为 FERV39k。我们从三个方面分析了构建这样一个新颖的数据集的重要组成部分：（1）多场景层次结构和表达类，（2）候选视频剪辑的生成，（3）可信的手动标记过程。基于这些指导方针，我们选择了4个场景，细分为22个场景，根据精心设计的工作流程对从4k视频中自动获得的86k样本进行注释，最终构建了38,935个带有7种经典表情标记的视频片段。还提供了四种基线框架的实验基准，并进一步分析了它们在不同场景下的性能以及未来研究的一些挑战。此外，我们通过消融研究系统地研究了 DFER 的关键组成部分。基线框架和我们的项目将可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为FERV39k的大规模多场景视频数据集，用于改进视频中面部表情识别（FER）的研究。
AI.motivation: 现有FER基准主要针对静态图像，缺乏真实场景下的视频数据集，难以评估方法在实际应用中的表现。
AI.method: 通过多场景分层设计、视频片段生成及可信人工标注流程，从4k视频中选取4个场景22个子场景，标注86k样本，构建包含38,935个片段的7类表情数据集，并测试基线框架性能。
AI.result: 提供了四种基线框架的实验基准，分析了不同场景下的性能差异，并通过消融实验探究DFER关键组件，明确了未来研究挑战。
AI.conclusion: FERV39k填补了视频FER数据集的空白，为跨场景表情识别研究提供了新基准，并指明了技术改进方向。

method: 通过多场景分层设计、视频片段生成及可信人工标注流程，从4k视频中选取4个场景22个子场景，标注86k样本，构建包含38,935个片段的7类表情数据集，并测试基线框架性能。
result: 提供了四种基线框架的实验基准，分析了不同场景下的性能差异，并通过消融实验探究DFER关键组件，明确了未来研究挑战。
conclusion: FERV39k填补了视频FER数据集的空白，为跨场景表情识别研究提供了新基准，并指明了技术改进方向。
search_text: FERV39k: A Large-Scale Multi-Scene Dataset for Facial Expression Recognition in Videos FERV39k：用于视频中面部表情识别的大规模多场景数据集 Current benchmarks for facial expression recognition (FER) mainly focus on static images, while there are limited datasets for FER in videos. It is still ambiguous to evaluate whether performances of existing methods remain satisfactory in real-world application-oriented scenes. For example, the "Happy" expression with high intensity in Talk-Show is more discriminating than the same expression with low intensity in Official-Event. To fill this gap, we build a large-scale multi-scene dataset, coined as FERV39k. We analyze the important ingredients of constructing such a novel dataset in three aspects: (1) multi-scene hierarchy and expression class, (2) generation of candidate video clips, (3) trusted manual labelling process. Based on these guidelines, we select 4 scenarios subdivided into 22 scenes, annotate 86k samples automatically obtained from 4k videos based on the well-designed workflow, and finally build 38,935 video clips labeled with 7 classic expressions. Experiment benchmarks on four kinds of baseline frameworks were also provided and further analysis on their performance across different scenes and some challenges for future research were given. Besides, we systematically investigate key components of DFER by ablation studies. The baseline framework and our project will be available. 目前面部表情识别（FER）的基准主要集中在静态图像，而视频中的 FER 数据集有限。评估现有方法的性能在现实世界的面向应用的场景中是否仍然令人满意仍然不明确。例如，脱口秀中高强度的“快乐”表达比官方活动中低强度的相同表达更具辨别力。为了填补这一空白，我们构建了一个大规模多场景数据集，命名为 FERV39k。我们从三个方面分析了构建这样一个新颖的数据集的重要组成部分：（1）多场景层次结构和表达类，（2）候选视频剪辑的生成，（3）可信的手动标记过程。基于这些指导方针，我们选择了4个场景，细分为22个场景，根据精心设计的工作流程对从4k视频中自动获得的86k样本进行注释，最终构建了38,935个带有7种经典表情标记的视频片段。还提供了四种基线框架的实验基准，并进一步分析了它们在不同场景下的性能以及未来研究的一些挑战。此外，我们通过消融研究系统地研究了 DFER 的关键组成部分。基线框架和我们的项目将可用。 本文提出一个名为FERV39k的大规模多场景视频数据集，用于改进视频中面部表情识别（FER）的研究。 Yan Wang,Yixuan Sun,Yiwen Huang,Zhongying Liu,Shuyong Gao,Wei Zhang,Weifeng Ge,Wenqiang Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Xuanchi Ren,Xiaolong Wang
title: Look Outside the Room: Synthesizing A Consistent Long-Term 3D Scene Video from A Single Image
title_zh: 看看房间外面：从单个图像合成一致的长期 3D 场景视频
abstract: Novel view synthesis from a single image has recently attracted a lot of attention, and it has been primarily advanced by 3D deep learning and rendering techniques. However, most work is still limited by synthesizing new views within relatively small camera motions. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to synthesize a consistent long-term video given a single scene image and a trajectory of large camera motions. Our approach utilizes an autoregressive Transformer to perform sequential modeling of multiple frames, which reasons the relations between multiple frames and the corresponding cameras to predict the next frame. To facilitate learning and ensure consistency among generated frames, we introduce a locality constraint based on the input cameras to guide self-attention among a large number of patches across space and time. Our method outperforms state-of-the-art view synthesis approaches by a large margin, especially when synthesizing long-term future in indoor 3D scenes. Project page at https://xrenaa.github.io/look-outside-room/.
summary: Novel view synthesis from a single image has recently attracted a lot of attention, and it has been primarily advanced by 3D deep learning and rendering techniques. However, most work is still limited by synthesizing new views within relatively small camera motions. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to synthesize a consistent long-term video given a single scene image and a trajectory of large camera motions. Our approach utilizes an autoregressive Transformer to perform sequential modeling of multiple frames, which reasons the relations between multiple frames and the corresponding cameras to predict the next frame. To facilitate learning and ensure consistency among generated frames, we introduce a locality constraint based on the input cameras to guide self-attention among a large number of patches across space and time. Our method outperforms state-of-the-art view synthesis approaches by a large margin, especially when synthesizing long-term future in indoor 3D scenes. Project page at https://xrenaa.github.io/look-outside-room/.
abstract_zh: 来自单个图像的新颖视图合成最近引起了很多关注，并且它主要是通过 3D 深度学习和渲染技术来推进的。然而，大多数工作仍然受到在相对较小的相机运动中合成新视图的限制。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的方法来在给定单个场景图像和大相机运动轨迹的情况下合成一致的长期视频。我们的方法利用自回归 Transformer 对多个帧执行顺序建模，这会推理多个帧与相应相机之间的关系来预测下一帧。为了促进学习并确保生成的帧之间的一致性，我们引入了基于输入摄像机的局部性约束，以指导跨空间和时间的大量补丁之间的自注意力。我们的方法大大优于最先进的视图合成方法，尤其是在室内 3D 场景中合成长期未来时。项目页面位于 https://xrenaa.github.io/look-outside-room/。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从单张图像合成一致的长期3D场景视频的方法，能够处理大范围相机运动轨迹。
AI.motivation: 现有视图合成方法受限于小范围相机运动，难以生成长期连续且一致的3D视频，尤其在复杂室内场景中存在明显不足。
AI.method: 采用自回归Transformer模型进行多帧序列建模，结合基于输入相机参数的局部性约束，通过时空patches的自注意力机制确保帧间一致性。
AI.result: 在室内3D场景的长期未来帧合成任务中显著优于现有最先进的视图合成方法，生成视频在视觉连续性和几何一致性上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入局部性约束的自回归Transformer架构，有效解决了大范围相机运动下的长期3D视频合成问题，为复杂场景的视频生成提供了新思路。

authors_text: Francesco Cappio Borlino,Silvia Bucci,Tatiana Tommasi
title: Contrastive Learning for Cross-Domain Open World Recognition
title_zh: 跨领域开放世界识别的对比学习
comment: This work has been accepted for publication at IROS 2022
comments: This work has been accepted for publication at IROS 2022
abstract: The ability to evolve is fundamental for any valuable autonomous agent whose knowledge cannot remain limited to that injected by the manufacturer. Consider for example a home assistant robot: it should be able to incrementally learn new object categories when requested, but also to recognize the same objects in different environments (rooms) and poses (hand-held/on the floor/above furniture), while rejecting unknown ones. Despite its importance, this scenario has started to raise interest in the robotic community only recently and the related research is still in its infancy, with existing experimental testbeds but no tailored methods. With this work, we propose the first learning approach that deals with all the previously mentioned challenges at once by exploiting a single contrastive objective. We show how it learns a feature space perfectly suitable to incrementally include new classes and is able to capture knowledge which generalizes across a variety of visual domains. Our method is endowed with a tailored effective stopping criterion for each learning episode and exploits a self-paced thresholding strategy that provides the classifier with a reliable rejection option. Both these novel contributions are based on the observation of the data statistics and do not need manual tuning. An extensive experimental analysis confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach in establishing the new state-of-the-art. The code is available at https://github.com/FrancescoCappio/Contrastive_Open_World.
summary: The ability to evolve is fundamental for any valuable autonomous agent whose knowledge cannot remain limited to that injected by the manufacturer. Consider for example a home assistant robot: it should be able to incrementally learn new object categories when requested, but also to recognize the same objects in different environments (rooms) and poses (hand-held/on the floor/above furniture), while rejecting unknown ones. Despite its importance, this scenario has started to raise interest in the robotic community only recently and the related research is still in its infancy, with existing experimental testbeds but no tailored methods. With this work, we propose the first learning approach that deals with all the previously mentioned challenges at once by exploiting a single contrastive objective. We show how it learns a feature space perfectly suitable to incrementally include new classes and is able to capture knowledge which generalizes across a variety of visual domains. Our method is endowed with a tailored effective stopping criterion for each learning episode and exploits a self-paced thresholding strategy that provides the classifier with a reliable rejection option. Both these novel contributions are based on the observation of the data statistics and do not need manual tuning. An extensive experimental analysis confirms the effectiveness of the proposed approach in establishing the new state-of-the-art. The code is available at https://github.com/FrancescoCappio/Contrastive_Open_World.
abstract_zh: 进化能力对于任何有价值的自主代理来说都是基础，其知识不能仅限于制造商注入的知识。以家庭助理机器人为例：它应该能够根据要求逐步学习新的物体类别，而且能够识别不同环境（房间）和姿势（手持/地板上/家具上方）中的相同物体，同时拒绝未知物体。尽管这种情况很重要，但直到最近才开始引起机器人界的兴趣，相关研究仍处于起步阶段，现有实验测试平台，但没有量身定制的方法。通过这项工作，我们提出了第一种学习方法，通过利用单个对比目标来一次性处理所有前面提到的挑战。我们展示了它如何学习完全适合增量包含新类的特征空间，并能够捕获跨各种视觉领域的知识。我们的方法为每个学习片段赋予了定制的有效停止标准，并利用自定进度的阈值策略，为分类器提供了可靠的拒绝选项。这两个新颖的贡献都是基于对数据统计的观察，不需要手动调整。广泛的实验分析证实了所提出的方法在建立新的最先进技术方面的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/FrancescoCappio/Contrastive_Open_World 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于对比学习的跨领域开放世界识别方法，解决自主代理在增量学习新类别、跨环境识别及拒绝未知对象的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏针对跨领域开放世界场景的专门方法，无法同时处理增量学习、跨域泛化和未知对象拒绝问题。
AI.method: 通过单一对比学习目标构建特征空间，结合数据统计的停止准则与自适应阈值策略，实现无需人工调参的增量学习与未知样本拒绝。
AI.result: 实验验证方法在跨领域开放世界任务中达到新SOTA，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了跨域开放世界的增量学习与未知拒绝难题，为自主代理的持续学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Amnon Geifman,Meirav Galun,David Jacobs,Ronen Basri
title: On the Spectral Bias of Convolutional Neural Tangent and Gaussian Process Kernels
title_zh: 卷积神经正切和高斯过程核的谱偏差
abstract: We study the properties of various over-parametrized convolutional neural architectures through their respective Gaussian process and neural tangent kernels. We prove that, with normalized multi-channel input and ReLU activation, the eigenfunctions of these kernels with the uniform measure are formed by products of spherical harmonics, defined over the channels of the different pixels. We next use hierarchical factorizable kernels to bound their respective eigenvalues. We show that the eigenvalues decay polynomially, quantify the rate of decay, and derive measures that reflect the composition of hierarchical features in these networks. Our results provide concrete quantitative characterization of over-parameterized convolutional network architectures.
summary: We study the properties of various over-parametrized convolutional neural architectures through their respective Gaussian process and neural tangent kernels. We prove that, with normalized multi-channel input and ReLU activation, the eigenfunctions of these kernels with the uniform measure are formed by products of spherical harmonics, defined over the channels of the different pixels. We next use hierarchical factorizable kernels to bound their respective eigenvalues. We show that the eigenvalues decay polynomially, quantify the rate of decay, and derive measures that reflect the composition of hierarchical features in these networks. Our results provide concrete quantitative characterization of over-parameterized convolutional network architectures.
abstract_zh: 我们通过各自的高斯过程和神经正切核来研究各种超参数化卷积神经架构的属性。我们证明，通过归一化多通道输入和 ReLU 激活，这些具有统一测量值的内核的本征函数是由在不同像素的通道上定义的球谐函数的乘积形成的。接下来，我们使用分层可分解内核来限制它们各自的特征值。我们证明特征值以多项式衰减，量化衰减率，并得出反映这些网络中层次特征组成的度量。我们的结果提供了过度参数化卷积网络架构的具体定量表征。

AI.tldr: 本文研究过参数化卷积神经网络的谱偏差，通过分析其对应的高斯过程核和神经切线核的特征函数及特征值衰减特性，提供定量描述。
AI.motivation: 理解卷积神经网络在图像任务中的优异性能需要从理论层面分析其谱偏差特性，尤其关注过参数化网络的特征学习机制。
AI.method: 通过高斯过程和神经切线核理论，证明归一化输入下特征函数由球面调和函数构成，并利用分层可分解核分析特征值衰减规律。
AI.result: 发现特征值呈多项式衰减并量化衰减速率，推导出反映分层特征组成的度量指标，实现对网络架构的定量表征。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为过参数化卷积网络提供了理论层面的定量分析框架，揭示了其分层特征学习与谱偏差之间的内在联系。

authors_text: Andri Bergsson,Søren Hauberg
title: Visualizing Riemannian data with Rie-SNE
title_zh: 使用 Rie-SNE 可视化黎曼数据
abstract: Faithful visualizations of data residing on manifolds must take the underlying geometry into account when producing a flat planar view of the data. In this paper, we extend the classic stochastic neighbor embedding (SNE) algorithm to data on general Riemannian manifolds. We replace standard Gaussian assumptions with Riemannian diffusion counterparts and propose an efficient approximation that only requires access to calculations of Riemannian distances and volumes. We demonstrate that the approach also allows for mapping data from one manifold to another, e.g. from a high-dimensional sphere to a low-dimensional one.
summary: Faithful visualizations of data residing on manifolds must take the underlying geometry into account when producing a flat planar view of the data. In this paper, we extend the classic stochastic neighbor embedding (SNE) algorithm to data on general Riemannian manifolds. We replace standard Gaussian assumptions with Riemannian diffusion counterparts and propose an efficient approximation that only requires access to calculations of Riemannian distances and volumes. We demonstrate that the approach also allows for mapping data from one manifold to another, e.g. from a high-dimensional sphere to a low-dimensional one.
abstract_zh: 在生成数据的平面视图时，流形上数据的忠实可视化必须考虑基础几何形状。在本文中，我们将经典的随机邻域嵌入（SNE）算法扩展到一般黎曼流形上的数据。我们用黎曼扩散对应物替换标准高斯假设，并提出一种仅需要计算黎曼距离和体积的有效近似。我们证明该方法还允许将数据从一个流形映射到另一个流形，例如从高维球体到低维球体。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种扩展的t-SNE算法（Rie-SNE），用于在考虑数据所在黎曼流形几何结构的前提下，将高维流形数据可视化为低维平面视图。
AI.motivation: 传统可视化方法在处理流形数据时忽视了其内在几何特性，导致无法准确反映数据的真实结构关系。
AI.method: 将标准SNE中的高斯分布假设替换为黎曼流形上的扩散过程，仅需计算黎曼距离和体积的高效近似方法，并实现跨流形的数据映射。
AI.result: 验证了该方法在保持流形几何特性的同时，能有效将数据从高维球面等流形映射到低维流形，且可视化效果优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: Rie-SNE通过引入黎曼几何特性，为流形数据的可视化提供了更精确的解决方案，并拓展了跨流形映射的可能性。
